name: Football for Peace Gambia
description: Football4Peace (F4P)  trains local coaches to deliver inclusive programmes for young people. Their model prioritises sustainability, environmental, economic, and human, so impact outlasts every visit. Through football, F4P builds bridges, breaks barriers, and hands ownership back to the community.

name: Football for Peace South Korea
description: Football4Peace (F4P)  trains local coaches to deliver inclusive programmes for young people. Their model prioritises sustainability, environmental, economic, and human, so impact outlasts every visit. Through football, F4P builds bridges, breaks barriers, and hands ownership back to the community.

name: Basketball Without Borders – South America
description: Basketball Without Borders brings NBA and WNBA players to South America to run camps for underserved youth, with a special focus on female participation. The programme combines basketball skills with leadership development and community service.

name: Streetfootball World Network
description: Streetfootball World connects a global network of grassroots organisations using football to empower marginalised communities, with a current focus on integration programmes for refugees across Europe. The Berlin hub coordinates advocacy and network activities.

name: The L'Ouverture Academy Sports School Project
description: The L'Ouverture Academy Sports School Project combines athletic development with academic opportunity to empower young Haitian football players. By providing structured educational infrastructure and a professional sports support network, the Academy offers youth a concrete pathway to higher education and a meaningful alternative to gang recruitment.

name: Zambia Community HIV Prevention Project
description: Sport in Action uses football and other sport activities to deliver HIV/AIDS awareness and sexual health education to young people in Zambia. It also aims to build capacity and develop leadership among adolescent girls and young women.

name: Right to Play – Nepal
description: Right to Play Nepal implements play-based learning programmes in schools and community centres, using sport and structured games to improve child wellbeing, education outcomes, and resilience. The programme trains local teachers and coaches as Play Leaders.

name: Peace and Sport
description: Peace and Sport is an international organisation that uses sport as a tool to promote peace in conflict and post-conflict zones worldwide. It works with athletes as ambassadors and coordinates field programmes in fragile states.

name: TAFISA – Sport for All
description: TAFISA promotes sport for all and active lifestyles globally, with a focus on accessible physical activity for people of all ages and backgrounds. The Brazil chapter organises community events integrating environmental education and sustainability practices.

name: Boys & Girls Sports Program
description: MYSA pioneered the use of football, table tennis and badminton as tools to encourage co-operation, raise self-esteem and promote physical and environmental health amongst the young people in the Mathare slums and its environs. In addition to playing matches every weekend, the MYSA teams undertake garbage clean-up and tree planting activities for which they earn league points.

name: Coaching for Life
description: The Arsenal Foundation has built pitches inside Za'atari refugee camp in Jordan to provide displaced children and youth with safe spaces in which to play and learn. Structured football programming aims to build vulnerable young people's mental, emotional and physical wellbeing.

name: Fight for Chicago
description: A nonprofit on Chicago's South Side using boxing and combat sports as a vehicle for personal development among youth. Professional coaches deliver structured training to empower young people facing adversity with tools for personal growth and leadership through mentoring, discipline, and competition.

name: Get into Rugby PLUS program
description: Get into Rugby PLUS is a flagship sport for development program that embeds life skills learning with rugby union to promote positive behaviour, gender equality, and prevent violence against women, girls and boys.

name: Special Olympics – European School Programme
description: An inclusive sport education programme integrating athletes with intellectual disabilities into mainstream school environments across Brussels. Coaches and educators are trained to deliver accessible physical activity sessions requiring no prior sport-specific experience, fostering social inclusion and active participation for all.

name: Sport Infrastructure Programme – Ma Balle - Ta Balle
description: The Ma Balle - Ta Balle association aims to make it easier for children in Mwanza (Tanzania) to participate in club sports and to rethink development cooperation. Every year, they build a sports and community center in Mwanza and support club sports throughout the region, which has a population of approximately 4 million. They focus on the four most popular sports in Tanzania: soccer, basketball, volleyball, and netball.
